title: primula latifolia subsp. ligustica
intro: Primula latifolia subsp. ligustica, also known as the Ligurian primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It is native to the Liguria region in northwest Italy, and its name is derived from this region. Primula latifolia subsp. ligustica has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often used in herbal remedies to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
